# `istream_clear()` {#sec-istream_clear}

`istream_clear()` resets both the reading and writing pointers of the given `istream_h` instance, effectively clearing its internal buffer.


::: {.panel-tabset}
## C


**Prototype**

```C
void istream_clear(
    istream_h istream
);
```

**Parameters**

| Key | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `istream` | `istream_h` | The input stream handle whose buffer will be cleared. |

---

**Return Value**

This function does not return a value.

**Notes**

This function is equivalent to calling [`istream_reset_rd()`](#sec-istream_reset_rd) and [`istream_reset_wr()`](#sec-istream_reset_wr) in sequence.


## JS


**Prototype**

````JS
// Not applicable in JS
````


## Python


**Prototype**

````Python
# Not applicable in Python
````


:::


::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Examples"}

::: {.panel-tabset}
## C


````C
// TODO C examples
````


## JS


````JS
// TODO JS examples
````


## Python


````python
# TODO Python examples
````


:::

:::
